title: EU Plans to Open Country Quotas for Certain Iron or Non-Alloy Steel Profiles from Türkiye
bodyHtml:
As known, the European Union (EU) applies a safeguard measure on steel products in the form of a 25% additional tariff and allocates country-specific quotas for exemption from this tariff based on product categories.

According to a WTO notification submitted by the EU on 11 June 2025, the EU is planning to open country-specific quotas for Category 17 steel products for Türkiye, the United Kingdom, and South Korea.

Category 17 covers "profiles of iron or non-alloy steel" classified under CN codes 7216 31 10, 7216 31 90, 7216 32 11, 7216 32 19, 7216 32 91, 7216 32 99, 7216 33 10, and 7216 33 90.

Of the 67,000-ton periodic quota previously allocated to "Other Countries," approximately 23,000 tons will be allocated to Türkiye, 27,500 tons to the United Kingdom, and 5,335 tons to South Korea as of 1 July.

To access the relevant notification, please click here.
